pi_items.source: Code analysis of setup_task.sh - Python script creates 5 messages (msg1, msg2, msg3, msg4, msg5) concatenated with \r\n delimiters
Code analysis of setup_task.sh - Each message's MSH segment field 10 (message ID field) contains these exact IDs in order
Code analysis of setup_task.sh lines 100 and 105 - directory created and batch file copied to this container path
Code analysis of setup_task.sh lines 101-102 - directory created in container with appropriate permissions

privileged_info_summary: The task processes synthetic HL7 v2.5.1 batch files containing exactly 5 ORU^R01 (Observation Result - Unsolicited) messages. The batch file (lab_batch_20240115.hl7) is generated by setup_task.sh and contains messages with IDs MSG001 through MSG005. The File Reader source reads from /var/hl7_input inside the NextGen Connect container, and output files are written to /var/hl7_output. Each input message contains standard HL7 segments (MSH, PID, OBR, OBX) with the message IDs located in the MSH segment field 10. The HL7 encoding characters are ^~\& (component, repetition, escape, subcomponent separators respectively) per the 2.5.1 standard.
